texto: I was a theater dork in high school and did all the plays. My theater teacher in high school, Janet Spahr, was absolutely incredible and mentored me throughout school. She taught me a lot about relying on my instincts.

texto: I was kind of like the Rhea Perlman of the bar. I was like Carla on 'Cheers.' People were more afraid of me. There was a point where I got a little surly. There were only so many chicken wings I could serve before losing the smile on my face.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melissa Rauch: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “I was kind of like the Rhea Perlman of the bar. I was like Carla on 'Cheers.' People were more af…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, losing, me pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de me e people.

frase_original: I was kind of like the Rhea Perlman of the bar. I was like Carla on 'Cheers.' People were more afraid of me. There was a point where I got a little surly. There were only so many chicken wings I could serve before losing the smile on my face.
